title_id: Hadis #28: Aku Mewasiatkan Kamu dengan Takwa (lanjutan)
content_en: The least point that one can make from this hadith is that the personal opinion that comes from the caliphs, if it is to be considered from [the Prophet's] sunnah as was stated, must take precedence over other personal opinion when no evidence is present.1 On the other hand, al-Sanaani takes a much more restricted approach. He stated that all what is meant by following the sunnah of the rightly-guided caliphs is to follow them in that which they follow the sunnah of the Prophet (peace be upon him), concerning jihad against the unbelievers, establishing the practices of the religion and so forth. 2 Another View Not everyone is of the opinion that the "rightly-guided successors" as mentioned in this hadith are only Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthmaan and Ali. Al-Qaari, for example, states that "it is said," that it is these four but later he says, "It is also said" that it refers to all of the scholars of Islam ( mujtahideen ), as they are the successors to the Prophet (peace be upon him) in that they revive the truth, guide the people and raise the banner of Islam. Al-Qaari himself does not state which view he prefers. 3 Al-Mudaabaghi states that the successors are first and foremost the Companions in general.4 The problem with this view is that it makes what the Prophet (peace be upon him) specifically told them almost meaningless. It would mean that the Prophet (peace be upon him) told his Companions, "You will see lots of differences, so follow my sunnah and your sunnah and stick to them . . . " Allah knows best, but this seems to be a strange bit of advice. One could understand him telling them that they should stick to the leaders of the Companions who are rightly-guided and sure to follow the way of the Prophet (peace be upon him) in their own personal rulings (ijtihaad). In his commentary to this hadith, al-Sindi makes no explicit mention of Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthmaan and Ali. He stated that the meaning of the rightly­ guided successors are the Imams who are followed and the respected scholars of deep knowledge.5 Hence, he is not restricting it to the above mentioned four caliphs. According to this understanding, during times of confusion and trials, one should follow the way of the Prophet (peace be upon him) as well as the way of the well-known accepted leaders and scholars of Islam. In his commentary to this hadith, ibn Uthaimeen wrote, 1 Quoted in al-Mubaarakfoori, vol. 7, pp. 440-441. 2 Quoted in al-Mubaarakfoori, vol. 3, pp. 50-51. 3 Al-Qaari, vol. I, pp. 242-243. 4 Al-Mudaabaghi, p. 220. 5 Al-Sindi, Sharh al-Arbaeen, p. 94. Hadith #28: "I advise you to have taqwa . . . " [The word] khulafaa is the plural of khaleejah: they are the people who succeed the Prophet (peace be upon him) among his Nation in knowledge, actions, calling to Islam, jihad and rule. At the head of them are the four rightly-guided caliphs, Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthmaan and Ali, may Allah be pleased with them. May we be joined with them in the gardens of pleasure. These four caliphs and the successors of the Nation who come after them, those who succeed the Prophet (peace be upon him) in this Nation, they are the ones whose paths we are ordered to follow. But one must realize that the sunnah of these successors comes after the sunnah of the Messenger (peace be upon him). If there is any conflict between the sunnah of any of these successors with the sunnah of the Prophet (peace be upon him), the rule is with the sunnah of Muhammad (peace be upon him) and with no one else. This is because their sunnah comes after or follows the sunnah of the Prophet (peace be upon him).1 As evidence for his last statement above, ibn Uthaimeen quotes ibn Abbaas who said, "Soon I would expect rocks to fall upon you from the sky when I say, 'The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said something,' and you answer, 'Abu Bakr and Umar said."' That is with respect to Abu Bakr and Umar. What then, says ibn Uthaimeen, must be the case with those who are less in stature than Abu Bakr and Umar?2 Elsewhere, while discussing the same point, ibn Uthaimeen stated that one must follow what the Prophet (peace be upon him) said and excuse any Companion who may have said anything that goes against what the Prophet (peace be upon him) said. Such a statement must be from his personal reasoning and judgment, for which he may be excused.3 Muhammad Al-Ashqar also does not agree that the rightly-guided successors are the four well-known caliphs. Al-Alaai had stated that there is a consensus that the rightly-guided caliphs are the four and only those four caliphs. Al-Ashqar wrote the following footnote to that statement of al-Alaai's, I do not know where that consensus came from.
content_id:
Poin paling minimal yang dapat diambil dari hadis ini adalah bahwa pendapat pribadi yang berasal dari para khalifah, jika dianggap sebagai bagian dari sunnah [Nabi ﷺ] sebagaimana dinyatakan, harus lebih diutamakan daripada pendapat pribadi lainnya ketika tidak ada bukti yang ada.

Di sisi lain, Ash-Shan'ani mengambil pendekatan yang jauh lebih terbatas. Beliau menyatakan bahwa semua yang dimaksud dengan mengikuti sunnah para khalifah yang mendapat petunjuk adalah mengikuti mereka dalam apa yang mereka ikuti dari sunnah Nabi ﷺ, mengenai jihad melawan orang-orang kafir, menegakkan praktik-praktik agama, dan sebagainya.

Pandangan Lain: Tidak semua orang berpendapat bahwa 'para penerus yang mendapat petunjuk' sebagaimana disebutkan dalam hadis ini hanyalah Abu Bakar, Umar, Utsman, dan Ali. Al-Qari, misalnya, menyatakan bahwa 'dikatakan' bahwa itu adalah keempat orang ini, tetapi kemudian beliau mengatakan 'juga dikatakan' bahwa itu merujuk kepada semua ulama Islam (mujtahidin), karena mereka adalah penerus Nabi ﷺ dalam menghidupkan kebenaran, membimbing orang-orang, dan mengibarkan panji Islam. Al-Qari sendiri tidak menyatakan pandangan mana yang beliau sukai.

Al-Mudabaghi menyatakan bahwa para penerus itu adalah pertama dan terutama para Sahabat secara umum. Masalah dengan pandangan ini adalah bahwa hal itu membuat apa yang secara khusus Nabi ﷺ katakan kepada mereka hampir tidak bermakna. Itu berarti Nabi ﷺ memberitahu para Sahabatnya: 'Kamu akan melihat banyak perbedaan, jadi ikutilah sunnahku dan sunnahmu dan berpeganglah pada mereka...' Wallahu a'lam, tetapi ini tampaknya merupakan saran yang aneh. Seseorang dapat memahami beliau memberitahu mereka bahwa mereka harus berpegang pada para pemimpin Sahabat yang mendapat petunjuk dan pasti mengikuti jalan Nabi ﷺ dalam ijtihad pribadi mereka.

Dalam komentarnya pada hadis ini, Al-Sindi tidak secara eksplisit menyebutkan Abu Bakar, Umar, Utsman, dan Ali. Beliau menyatakan bahwa makna para penerus yang mendapat petunjuk adalah para imam yang diikuti dan ulama-ulama yang dihormati dengan pengetahuan yang mendalam. Dengan demikian, beliau tidak membatasinya pada keempat khalifah yang disebutkan di atas. Menurut pemahaman ini, selama masa kebingungan dan ujian, seseorang harus mengikuti jalan Nabi ﷺ serta jalan para pemimpin dan ulama Islam yang dikenal dan diterima.

Dalam komentarnya pada hadis ini, Ibn Utsaimin menulis: 'Kata khulafa' adalah bentuk jamak dari khalifah: mereka adalah orang-orang yang menggantikan Nabi ﷺ di antara umatnya dalam ilmu, amal, dakwah kepada Islam, jihad, dan pemerintahan. Di kepala mereka adalah empat khalifah yang mendapat petunjuk, Abu Bakar, Umar, Utsman, dan Ali, semoga Allah meridhai mereka. Semoga kita bergabung dengan mereka di taman-taman kesenangan. Keempat khalifah ini dan para penerus umat yang datang setelah mereka, mereka yang menggantikan Nabi ﷺ dalam umat ini, merekalah orang-orang yang jalannya kita perintahkan untuk diikuti. Namun seseorang harus menyadari bahwa sunnah para penerus ini datang setelah sunnah Rasulullah ﷺ. Jika ada konflik antara sunnah salah satu penerus ini dengan sunnah Nabi ﷺ, hukumnya ada pada sunnah Muhammad ﷺ dan bukan pada siapa pun.'

Sebagai bukti pernyataan terakhirnya di atas, Ibn Utsaimin mengutip Ibn Abbas yang berkata: 'Aku hampir berharap batu-batu akan jatuh menimpamu dari langit ketika aku berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda sesuatu,' dan kamu menjawab: 'Abu Bakar dan Umar berkata.' Itu sehubungan dengan Abu Bakar dan Umar. Lalu bagaimana, kata Ibn Utsaimin, haruslah keadaan mereka yang lebih rendah statusnya daripada Abu Bakar dan Umar?
content_ar: The least point that one can make from this الحديث is that the personal opinion that comes from the caliphs, إذا it is to be considered from [النبي ﷺ's] السنة as was stated, must take precedence over other personal opinion عندما no evidence is present.1 من ناحية أخرى, al-Sanaani takes a much more restricted approach. أفاد that all what is meant by following the السنة of the rightly-guided caliphs is للاتباع them in that which they follow the السنة of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ), فيما يتعلق بـ jihad against the unbelievers, establishing the practices of the religion and لذا forth. 2 Another View Not everyone is of the opinion that the "rightly-guided التابعين" as mentioned in this الحديث are only أبو بكر, عمر, Uthmaan and علي. Al-Qaari, على سبيل المثال, states that "قيل," that it is these four but later he says, "It is أيضاً said" that it refers to all of the العلماء of الإسلام ( mujtahideen ), as they are the التابعين to النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) in that they revive the الحق, guide the people and raise the banner of الإسلام. Al-Qaari himself does not state which view he prefers. 3 Al-Mudaabaghi states that the التابعين are أولاً and foremost the الصحابة in general.4 The problem with this view is that it makes what النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) specifically told them almost meaningless. It would mean that النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) told his الصحابة, "You will see lots of differences, لذا follow my السنة and your السنة and stick to them . . . " الله knows best, but this seems to be a strange bit of advice. One could understand him telling them that they should stick to the leaders of the الصحابة who are rightly-guided and sure للاتباع the الطريق of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) in their own personal rulings (ijtihaad). In his commentary to this الحديث, al-Sindi makes no explicit mention of أبو بكر, عمر, Uthmaan and علي. أفاد that the meaning of the rightly­ guided التابعين are the Imams who are followed and the respected العلماء of deep العلم.5 ومن ثَم, he is not restricting it to the above mentioned four caliphs. وفقاً لـ this الفهم, خلال times of confusion and الابتلاءات, ينبغي للمرء follow the الطريق of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) as well as the الطريق of the well-known accepted leaders and العلماء of الإسلام. In his commentary to this الحديث, ibn Uthaimeen wrote, 1 Quoted in al-Mubaarakfoori, vol. 7, pp. 440-441. 2 Quoted in al-Mubaarakfoori, vol. 3, pp. 50-51. 3 Al-Qaari, vol. I, pp. 242-243. 4 Al-Mudaabaghi, p. 220. 5 Al-Sindi, Sharh al-Arbaeen, p. 94. الحديث #28: "I advise you to have التقوى . . . " [The word] khulafaa is the plural of khaleejah: they are the people who succeed النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) among his Nation in العلم, actions, calling to الإسلام, jihad and rule. At the head of them are the four rightly-guided caliphs, أبو بكر, عمر, Uthmaan and علي, may الله be pleased with them. May we be joined with them in the gardens of pleasure. These four caliphs
